docs.title: Welcome to Product Documentation
docs.text: Product is a powerful platform that helps teams build, ship, and scale with confidence. This documentation covers everything from initial setup to advanced configuration.

docs.title: Quick Navigation
docs.text:
• Getting Started — Install and configure Product in minutes. Step-by-step guide to get up and running quickly.
  • Features — Explore real-time collaboration, analytics, and integrations.
  • Video Demo — Watch a walkthrough of key workflows and see Product in action.
  • Export to PDF — Build the full docs as a single PDF (site/pdf/document.pdf).

docs.title: What is Product?
docs.text:
Product is designed around three core principles:

SimplicityReliabilityExtensibility

Set up in under 5 minutes. No complex configuration required — sensible defaults handle 90% of use cases out of the box.

Built on battle-tested infrastructure with 99.9% uptime SLA. Automatic failover and replication keep your data safe.

A rich plugin ecosystem and open API allow you to integrate with any tool in your stack.

docs.title: Key Highlights
docs.text:
Feature Starter Pro Enterprise Users 5 50 Unlimited Storage 10 GB 100 GB Custom API Rate Limit 1,000 req/min 10,000 req/min Unlimited SSO / SAML Priority Support PDF Export

New in v2.0

Version 2.0 introduces native PDF export, dark mode support, and a redesigned dashboard. See the changelog for full details.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: Product ships with a broad set of built-in capabilities. This page gives you a high-level tour of each major feature area.

docs.title: Real-Time Collaboration
docs.text: Multiple team members can work on the same project simultaneously. Changes are broadcast over a WebSocket connection with operational-transform conflict resolution — no more "who saved last wins."

docs.title: Presence Indicators
docs.text:
See exactly who is viewing or editing a resource in real time:

  • Green dot — currently active
  • Yellow dot — idle for more than 2 minutes
  • Gray dot — viewing but not editing

docs.title: Comments & Annotations
docs.text:
Attach threaded comments to any resource, line of configuration, or pipeline step. Comments support Markdown and can mention @teammates to send notifications.

Keyboard shortcut

Press ++cmd+shift+m++ on macOS (or ++ctrl+shift+m++ on Windows/Linux) to open the comment panel from anywhere in the UI.

docs.title: Analytics Dashboard
docs.text: The built-in analytics dashboard provides real-time and historical metrics without requiring a separate observability stack.

docs.title: Available Metrics
docs.text: Metric Description Retention Request rate Requests per second across all endpoints 90 days Error rate Percentage of 4xx/5xx responses 90 days P99 latency 99th percentile response time 30 days Resource usage CPU, memory, and storage per resource 14 days Pipeline throughput Records processed per minute 30 days

docs.title: Custom Dashboards
docs.text:
Create custom dashboards by combining any available metrics into a grid layout:

# dashboard.yml
name: "Pipeline Health"
panels:
  - type: timeseries
    title: Throughput (records/min)
    metric: pipeline.throughput
    window: 1h

  - type: stat
    title: Error Rate (last 5m)
    metric: pipeline.error_rate
    threshold:
      warn: 0.01
      critical: 0.05

  - type: heatmap
    title: Latency Distribution
    metric: pipeline.latency_p99
    window: 24h

docs.title: Integrations
docs.text:
Product connects natively with the tools your team already uses.

Integration Description :simple-slack: Slack Send notifications to channels or DMs when pipelines fail, succeed, or need review. GitHub Trigger pipeline runs on pull requests, push events, or tag creation via GitHub Actions. :simple-amazonaws: AWS Read from S3, write to RDS, invoke Lambda, or stream from Kinesis with zero-config auth via IAM roles. Google Cloud Ingest from BigQuery, Pub/Sub, and Cloud Storage. Authenticate with Workload Identity. Datadog Forward all Product metrics and logs directly to your Datadog account. Webhooks Send HTTP POST events to any endpoint for custom integrations. Includes HMAC signature verification.

docs.title: Access Control
docs.text: Role-based access control (RBAC) is available on all plans.

docs.title: Built-in Roles
docs.text:
Owner
├── Admin
│   ├── Editor
│   │   └── Viewer
│   └── Billing Manager
└── API Service Account

docs.title: Custom Roles (Enterprise)
docs.text:
Define granular permissions with a policy language:

{
  "role": "pipeline-operator",
  "permissions": [
    { "resource": "pipeline", "actions": ["read", "run", "stop"] },
    { "resource": "log",      "actions": ["read"] },
    { "resource": "dataset",  "actions": ["read"] }
  ]
}

Permission inheritance

Permissions are additive — a user with both Viewer and pipeline-operator roles has the union of both sets of permissions.

docs.title: Data Export
docs.text:
Export any dataset or pipeline output in multiple formats:

CSVParquetJSON LinesPDF Report 
product export my-dataset --format csv --output ./data.csv

product export my-dataset --format parquet --output ./data.parquet

product export my-dataset --format jsonl --output ./data.jsonl

# Export a formatted PDF report of a resource's summary page
product report my-dataset --format pdf --output ./report.pdf

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Video Demo — watch these features in action
  • Installation — set up your environment

docs.title: Video Demo
docs.text:
Watch these walkthroughs to see Product in action. All recordings are available locally in the docs/assets/videos/ directory.

Adding your own videos

Place .mp4 files in docs/assets/videos/ and reference them with the embed code shown below. Videos are served as static assets — no external hosting required.

docs.title: Quickstart Walkthrough
docs.text:
This 3-minute overview covers creating your first workspace, running a pipeline, and inspecting the results.

Your browser does not support HTML5 video. Download the video instead.

What you'll see:

  1. Logging in and creating a workspace
  2. Importing a sample dataset from S3
  3. Running the built-in validation pipeline
  4. Reviewing results in the analytics dashboard

docs.title: Pipeline Configuration Deep-Dive
docs.text:
A 6-minute walkthrough covering advanced pipeline configuration, including multi-step transforms, conditional branching, and error handling strategies.

Your browser does not support HTML5 video. Download the video instead.

Topics covered:

  • Writing multi-step pipeline YAML
  • Using environment variable interpolation
  • Configuring retries and dead-letter queues
  • Connecting a Slack webhook for failure alerts

docs.title: How to Embed a Local Video
docs.text:
Copy this snippet into any Markdown page and adjust the src path:

<video
  controls
  width="100%"
  style="border-radius: 8px; max-width: 800px; display: block; margin: 1rem auto;"
  poster="path/to/thumbnail.png"
>
  <source src="path/to/your-video.mp4" type="video/mp4">
  Your browser does not support HTML5 video.
</video>

docs.title: Path Reference Guide
docs.text:
Page location Video in assets/videos/ Correct src docs/index.md quickstart.mp4 assets/videos/quickstart.mp4 docs/features/video-demo.md quickstart.mp4 ../assets/videos/quickstart.mp4 docs/getting-started/index.md quickstart.mp4 ../assets/videos/quickstart.mp4

Thumbnail / poster frame

The poster attribute sets the image shown before the user hits play. Point it at any PNG or JPG in your assets folder.

docs.title: Supported Formats
Getting Started
docs.text: This section walks you through everything you need to know to start using Product — from understanding the core concepts to configuring your first project.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Product follows a workspace → project → resource hierarchy. Understanding this model makes the rest of the documentation much clearer.

Workspace
├── Project A
│   ├── Resources
│   └── Settings
└── Project B
    ├── Resources
    └── Settings

docs.title: Workspaces
docs.text:
A workspace is the top-level container for your organization. It holds all projects, members, and billing information. You typically have one workspace per company or team.

Workspace limits

On the Starter plan, each workspace is limited to 5 members. Upgrade to Pro or Enterprise to add more collaborators.

docs.title: Projects
docs.text:
Projects are isolated environments within a workspace. Each project has its own:

  • Configuration and environment variables
  • Access control (invite specific members)
  • Resource quotas
  • Audit logs

docs.title: Resources
docs.text: Resources are the actual entities within a project — datasets, pipelines, models, or any custom type registered via the plugin API.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
Product uses token-based authentication. Every API call requires a bearer token:

curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <YOUR_TOKEN>" \
     https://api.example.com/v1/projects

Tokens are scoped to a workspace and can be restricted to specific permissions:

Scope Description read Read-only access to all resources write Create and update resources admin Full control including member management billing Access to billing and usage data

docs.title: Configuration File
docs.text:
Every project can include a product.yml configuration file at its root:

version: "2"
project:
  name: my-project
  region: us-east-1

resources:
  - name: primary-dataset
    type: dataset
    source: s3://my-bucket/data/

  - name: inference-pipeline
    type: pipeline
    steps:
      - transform
      - validate
      - export

settings:
  retention_days: 30
  notifications:
    slack_webhook: ${SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL}

docs.title: Event System
docs.text:
Product emits events for every significant action. You can subscribe to events via webhooks or the streaming API:

import product_sdk as product

client = product.Client(token="<YOUR_TOKEN>")

@client.on("resource.created")
def handle_created(event):
    print(f"New resource: {event.resource.name}")

@client.on("pipeline.failed")
def handle_failure(event):
    alert_team(event.error)

client.listen()

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: The diagram below shows how components interact at runtime:

docs.title: Data Flow
docs.text:
1. Ingestion Layer — accepts data from HTTP, message queues, or scheduled batch jobs
  2. Processing Engine — applies transformations, validations, and enrichments
  3. Storage Layer — persists results in columnar format with automatic indexing
  4. Query API — exposes a SQL-compatible interface for downstream consumers

docs.title: Fault Tolerance
docs.text:
Each layer implements independent retry logic with exponential backoff. If the processing engine becomes unavailable, the ingestion layer buffers incoming data in a durable queue for up to 24 hours.

Data retention

Buffered data older than 24 hours is dropped by default. Configure buffer.max_age to increase this limit.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Installation guide — install the CLI and SDK
  • Features overview — explore what Product can do
  • Video walkthrough — see it in action

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Follow these steps to install and verify your local development environment.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.9 or later
  • Node.js 18 or later (for the web dashboard)
  • Docker 24 or later (optional, for local service emulation)

docs.title: Install the CLI
docs.text:
macOS / LinuxWindowsDocker 
curl -sSL https://install.example.com | sh

Or via Homebrew:

brew tap example/product
brew install product

Download the installer from the releases page or use winget:

winget install Example.Product

docker pull example/product-cli:latest
docker run --rm example/product-cli:latest product --version

Verify the installation:

product --version
# product v2.0.1 (darwin/arm64)

docs.title: Install the Python SDK
docs.text:
pip install product-sdk

For development extras (testing utilities, type stubs):

pip install "product-sdk[dev]"

docs.title: Authenticate
docs.text:
product auth login

This opens a browser window where you complete the OAuth flow. Your token is stored in ~/.product/credentials.

To authenticate non-interactively (CI environments):

export PRODUCT_TOKEN=your_token_here
product auth verify

docs.title: Initialize a Project
docs.text:
mkdir my-project && cd my-project
product init

You'll be prompted to choose a workspace and project name. This creates a product.yml in the current directory.

You're ready

At this point you have a working installation. Head to the Features section to start exploring what Product can do.

docs.title: Building the Documentation as PDF
docs.text:
This documentation site is configured with the mkdocs-with-pdf plugin. After cloning the docs repo:

# Activate the virtual environment
source venv/bin/activate

# Build — PDF is generated at site/pdf/document.pdf
mkdocs build

# Or serve locally (PDF is NOT generated in serve mode)
mkdocs serve

The generated PDF will be at site/pdf/document.pdf.
